evals.prompt: I have an idea for a voice journaling app that turns daily reflections into weekly insight summaries. Draft it as an idea brief and save it in this repo.
evals.expected_output: A new idea brief markdown file saved under docs/ideas with the required front matter and all required sections filled with concise, concrete content.
assertions: The run saves exactly one new markdown file under docs/ideas named idea-<five digits>-<slug>.md
The saved file includes front matter keys id, type, role, status, and parent
The saved file uses status draft when the user did not request a different status
The saved file contains every required heading in the correct order
No scaffold prompt text remains in the saved file

evals.prompt: Create an idea brief for a procurement copilot that helps finance teams compare vendor quotes. Save it as active status and use parent idea-00010-vendor-ops.
evals.expected_output: A new idea brief markdown file saved under docs/ideas with status active, parent idea-00010-vendor-ops, and the standard idea brief structure filled in.
assertions: The saved file front matter sets status to active
The saved file front matter sets parent to idea-00010-vendor-ops
The risks section contains exactly three concrete risk bullets
The decision block keeps the three unchecked decision lines
The document stays concise rather than expanding into a long spec

evals.prompt: save this idea in docs/ideas: an sms concierge for independent restaurants that texts guests when their table is ready and handles simple waitlist updates
evals.expected_output: A concise idea brief saved under docs/ideas even though the prompt is informal and does not explicitly say idea brief.
assertions: The saved file still uses the standard idea brief headings and front matter
The one-line summary and proposed solution are specific to restaurant waitlist texting rather than generic chatbot wording
The current alternatives section names realistic existing options
The validator script would pass on the saved file
The output reports the saved path back to the user
